settingsSoundDescription: Play sound effects for launches, scoring, portals, tasks, and upgrade purchases.
settingsCopyProgressDescription: Copy a short English progress summary to paste into the feedback questionnaire later.
settingsCopyProgressManual: Clipboard copy was blocked, so a manual copy dialog was opened.
settingsCopyProgressManualPrompt: Clipboard copy was blocked. Copy this progress summary manually:
settingsResetProgressConfirm: Reset all saved progress and return to the start?
editorLockedDescription: The editor unlocks after you finish the intro checks.
editorUnlockedDescription: Use the goal above the editor as your target. Run when the code matches it.
helperEditorLockedDescription: The helper editor unlocks in the functions topic.
helperEditorUnlockedDescription: Store one reusable tactic here and call it from the main program.
helperEditorOptionalDescription: Optional here: you can still use the helper, but this topic can also be solved in the main program alone.
helperEditorCollapsedDescription: One reusable tactic is stored here. Expand it if you want to edit the helper.
programZeroStepMessage: Valid code, but it launches nothing yet.
programZeroStepNeedActionMessage: Valid code, but it launches nothing yet because this run never reaches 'drop_ball()' or 'skip_ball()'.
programZeroStepUnhandledBallMessage: Valid code, but it launches nothing yet because the next ball is '{ball}' and this code does not handle that case yet.
programZeroStepWrongBallMessage: Valid code, but it launches nothing yet because the next ball is '{ball}' and this code only reacts to '{handled}'.
runBlockedInvalidProgram: Fix the program before running it again.
runPanelRunningMessage: The machine is resolving the current run.
programErrorEmpty: Your program is empty. Add at least one drop_ball().
programErrorTooManyLines: Too many non-empty lines. The current limit is {limit}.
programErrorInvalidLine: Line {line}: this line is not valid right now.
programErrorDropBallArgs: Line {line}: drop_ball() does not take an input number. Choose the input first with choose_input(...), then call drop_ball().
programErrorUnsupportedForLoop: Line {line}: use 'for name in range(N):', 'for name in range(len(bonus_map)):', or 'for name in bonus_map:'.
programErrorForRangeLimit: Line {line}: range() must stay between 1 and {max}.
programErrorForBodyRequired: Line {line}: add at least one indented line under this block.
programErrorInvalidForBody: Line {line}: this line is not allowed inside the current loop body.
programErrorNestedBlocks: Line {line}: nested blocks are not available yet.
programErrorUnexpectedIndentation: Line {line}: this indented line needs a supported block above it.
programErrorStepLimitExceeded: This program would launch too many balls at once. The current limit is {max}.
programErrorUnknownIdentifier: Line {line}: '{name}' does not have a value yet. Set it before you use it.
programErrorInvalidExpression: Line {line}: this expression is not valid.
programErrorInvalidCondition: Line {line}: this condition is not valid.
programErrorInvalidIndexAccess: Line {line}: this list index is not valid.
programErrorInvalidSetAim: Line {line}: choose_input(...) needs one numeric expression.
programErrorContinueOutsideLoop: Line {line}: continue can only be used inside a for loop.
programErrorAimRangeLimit: Line {line}: choose_input(...) must stay between 1 and 3.
programErrorInvalidFunctionDefinition: Line {line}: helper functions must use def name(): in the helper editor.
programErrorHelperLimitExceeded: You can define up to {limit} helper function right now.
programErrorHelperLineLimitExceeded: The helper editor can use up to {limit} non-empty lines right now.
programErrorHelperNotDefined: Line {line}: helper function {name} does not exist yet.
availableStructuresEmpty: No Python structures are unlocked yet.
availableSyntaxStepLimit: Up to {limit} balls can be launched in one run.
referenceNoExamples: New examples appear here as topics unlock.
referenceBonusLaneDescription: Which third of the board is boosted right now.
referenceBonusMapDescription: 'bonus_map' is a list of input multipliers. Index 0 is input 1, index 1 is input 2, and index 2 is input 3.
referenceBonusMapExample:
If the multipliers are [1, 0.5, 5]:
bonus_map[0] = 1
bonus_map[1] = 0.5
bonus_map[2] = 5
referencePortalSideDescription: The board shows which side the portal is on. Python reads the same value as 'portal_side'.
referencePortalSideExample:
If the portal is left: portal_side = 1
If the portal is right: portal_side = 3
referenceNextBallDescription: 'next_ball' shows which ball would drop next. You can use it in conditionals.
referenceNextBallExample:
Center ball: next_ball = center_ball
Portal ball: next_ball = portal_ball
Negative ball: next_ball = negative_ball
referenceNormalBallDescription: 'center_ball' gives bonus points if you choose input 2 before dropping it.
referenceLuckyBallDescription: 'portal_ball' creates a stronger portal split: 4 balls instead of 2.
referenceEvilBallDescription: 'negative_ball' costs points. Usually you should skip it with 'skip_ball()'.
nextStepOnboardingBody:
Press 'Run program' to see what the current code does.
- It drops a ball from the default input 2.
nextStepCheckpointReadyBody: {topic} is ready for a short checkpoint. Pass the 3 tasks to unlock the next topic.
nextStepCheckpointActiveBody: Finish the current {topic} checkpoint task to keep progressing.
nextStepCompletedBody:
The guided path is complete. You can keep using the board as a sandbox: optimize your code, test auto-run, chase stronger multipliers, and try optional upgrades.
Thank you for playing.
helpCoachmarkBody: If you ever need help, press Help and skim the topic notes here.
referenceCoachmarkBody: Reference keeps your unlocked functions, board values, and example patterns together. Peek here whenever the syntax feels fuzzy.
helpCenterEmpty: The latest guides will appear here.
guideWelcomeBody: In CodePachinko, you write code to drop balls, choose inputs, skip some balls, and earn points. Start with the demo run and use these notes when a topic gets tricky.
currentGoalBoardMeaningLabel: Why it matters on the board
currentGoalOnboardingGoal: Watch one safe run, understand the basic board, and unlock the editor.
currentGoalOnboardingAction: Run the starter program once, then clear the onboarding checks.
currentGoalCompletedGoal: The guided modules now work together from variables through 'bonus_map'. The board is open for sandbox play.
currentGoalCompletedAction: Optimize your strategy: combine assignments, conditionals, helpers, loops, auto-run, upgrades, and 'bonus_map' to improve repeated runs.
currentGoalCompletedBoardMeaning: Better code and optional upgrades make repeated runs stronger.
currentGoalCheckpointAction: Start the checkpoint. Passing all 3 tasks installs {unlock} immediately.
currentGoalCheckpointActiveAction: Finish the open checkpoint task. Passing all 3 tasks installs {unlock} immediately.
currentGoalSpotlightAction: Read the primer cards first, then start the goal when you are ready to try it yourself.
boardSubtitle: Write code, run it, and watch the machine react.
boardGateExplanation: Aim at the portal to split balls into 2.
boardGateExplanationAdvanced: Aim at the portal to split balls. Most balls split into 2. 'portal_ball' splits into 4.
boardRandomnessExplanation: Your code chooses the input. The bounce after that is physics.
boardRandomnessExplanationAdvanced: Your code chooses the input and whether the ball launches. The bounce after that is physics.
boardLegendBonusHelp: Bonus lane = the lane that is boosted right now.
taskValidationNeedsRun: This answer still does not produce the required behavior.
taskValidationExpectedBehavior: Your code must handle these visible cases.
taskFeedbackWrongChute: This launches from input {actual}, but this task needs input {expected}.
taskFeedbackNeedChooseChute: Choose the input first with 'choose_input(...)' before you launch.
taskFeedbackNeedDropBall: The program still needs 'drop_ball()' to launch the ball.
taskFeedbackNeedVariableStore: Use 'portal_side' to read where the portal is. You can put it directly in an expression or store it in a variable first.
taskFeedbackNeedSkipEvil: This task needs a branch that skips 'negative_ball' with 'skip_ball()'.
taskFeedbackNeedSkipNegativeHelper: Define 'skip_negative()' and have it use 'skip_ball()' once for the visible 'negative_ball'.
taskFeedbackNeedElseLaunch: Negative balls should be skipped, but the launch branch still needs to handle the other balls.
taskFeedbackNeedPortalLaunch: Portal balls still need a launch branch that aims at the portal.
taskFeedbackNeedCenterLaunch: Center balls still need a launch branch that launches from input 2.
taskFeedbackNeedHelperCall: This task should use 'follow_portal()' instead of repeating the launch directly.
taskFeedbackNeedHelperGateLogic: The helper must choose the correct input and launch exactly once.
taskFeedbackNeedBestTracker: Track the current best multiplier and the input index it belongs to in your own variables before you choose an input.
taskFeedbackNeedContinue: After 'skip_ball()' or the center-ball launch, use 'continue' so that loop step stops at the right time.
taskFeedbackNeedFullPreviewLoop: This preview shows {expected} balls. Increase the loop count to handle all {expected}.
taskFeedbackWrongLaunchCount: This answer launches {actual} time(s), but this task needs {expected}.
activityFeedEmpty: Unlocks, solved tasks, and upgrades will appear here.
shopAvailableHint: Earn a few more points to buy this upgrade.
boardSubtitleOnboarding: Start with one demo run and watch how one ball moves through the board.
introChecklistTwo: Watch the code and try to understand what happened in the game.
introChecklistThree: After the run, checkpoint tasks will open. Try to understand them.
introChecklistFour: After the checkpoint tasks, read the new task above the code editor.
nextStepOnboardingAfterRunBody:
You have seen the demo program.
Solve two short tasks and after that you can start writing your own code.
nextStepOnboardingTaskBody: Finish the remaining quick check so you can start writing your own program.
nextStepUnlockBody: A new tool is ready. Read the short primer first, then start the goal when you are ready.
nextStepCompleteBody:
The guided path is complete. You can keep using the board as a sandbox: optimize your code, test auto-run, chase stronger multipliers, and try optional upgrades.
Thank you for playing.
goalEditRequiredMainMessage: This goal only counts after you edit the main program.
goalEditRequiredHelperMessage: This goal only counts after you edit the helper program.
boardPreviewMeaningNormal: Center ball: input 2 gives a +6 bonus.
boardPreviewMeaningLucky: Portal ball: send it to the portal for a 4-ball split.
boardPreviewMeaningEvil: Negative ball: launching it costs 10 points, so skip it.
autoRunReadyLabel: Auto-run is ready when you want to repeat this loop.
editorSpotlightDescription: Read the new concept first. Editing unlocks after you click Start goal.
helperEditorSpotlightDescription: Read the new concept first. The helper becomes editable after you click Start goal.
runPanelSpotlightMessage: Read the lesson above first and then click 'Start goal'.
taskFeedbackNeedList: Use 'bonus_map[index]' or loop directly with 'for multiplier in bonus_map:'. 'bonus_map[0]', '[1]', and '[2]' match inputs 1, 2, and 3.
